response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web developer with 15+ years of experience, but he is critically misaligned with this role on multiple hard requirements. Most significantly, the role mandates native-level Japanese proficiency, which Bogdan does not possess—his listed languages are English, Romanian, and German. Beyond the language barrier, he has no discernible background in network security (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), Layer-2/3 networking, LAN-switch firmware, or Linux kernel modification. His C/C++ exposure is limited to a single 6-month role at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project. His career has been almost entirely in web application development using TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, making him a poor fit for this embedded networking security position.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience across multiple domains, well exceeding the 3-year minimum
Demonstrated C/C++ usage at National Instruments and in embedded-adjacent work (Linux audio system for thesis)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, code quality focus, open-source contributions
concerns: Does not meet the native-level Japanese language requirement—a stated hard prerequisite
No experience with network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) or LAN-switch/embedded firmware development
C proficiency is minimal and dated; core expertise is TypeScript/Node.js/web, not systems or embedded programming
